system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"gate.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Availability of the service as measured by successful healthcheck responses.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.healthcheck.200"],"segment_dimension":"service_instance","rationale":"A basic liveness signal; drops indicate the service is down, wedged, or failing to serve requests."},{"key":"gate.api_success_rate_overall","description":"Overall successful API response rate across core todo endpoints (list/get/create/update/delete).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.list.200","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.create.201","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.get.404.todo_not_found","http.todos.create.400.title_required","http.todos.update.400.no_fields_to_update","http.todos.update.404.todo_not_found","http.todos.delete.404.todo_not_found","http.global_error.500.internal_server_error"],"segment_dimension":"endpoint","rationale":"Tracks end-to-end API reliability; a falling success ratio indicates regressions, outages, or increased server-side failures."},{"key":"gate.server_error_rate_500","description":"Rate of unhandled internal server errors returned to clients.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.global_error.500.internal_server_error"],"segment_dimension":"endpoint","rationale":"500s represent defects or infrastructure issues; monitoring rate helps catch crashes, bad deploys, and dependency failures quickly."},{"key":"gate_stacktrace_to_500_ratio","description":"Ratio of stacktrace logs to 500 responses (should correlate closely).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["log.error.stacktrace","http.global_error.500.internal_server_error"],"segment_dimension":"service_instance","rationale":"Detects logging gaps (missing stacktraces) or noisy logging (stacktraces without corresponding 500s), both of which hinder incident response."},{"key":"gate.todo_get_not_found_ratio","description":"Proportion of GET-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.404.todo_not_found","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404.todo_not_found"],"segment_dimension":"endpoint","rationale":"Elevated not-found ratios can indicate client misuse, broken ID propagation, data loss, or routing issues."},{"key":"gate.todo_update_not_found_ratio","description":"Proportion of UPDATE-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.404.todo_not_found","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.404.todo_not_found"],"segment_dimension":"endpoint","rationale":"Spikes can indicate stale clients, incorrect IDs, or data consistency problems affecting updates."},{"key":"gate.todo_delete_not_found_ratio","description":"Proportion of DELETE-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.404.todo_not_found","http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404.todo_not_found"],"segment_dimension":"endpoint","rationale":"Helps detect client retries against already-deleted items, incorrect IDs, or data integrity issues."},{"key":"gate.todo_create_validation_error_ratio","description":"Proportion of create requests rejected due to missing/blank title.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.400.title_required","http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.title_required"],"segment_dimension":"client_id","rationale":"High validation error ratios often indicate UI/API contract regressions, bot traffic, or misuse by specific clients."},{"key":"gate.todo_update_validation_error_ratio","description":"Proportion of update requests rejected because no updatable fields were provided.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.400.no_fields_to_update","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields_to_update"],"segment_dimension":"client_id","rationale":"Catches client-side bugs where empty payloads are sent, and helps pinpoint problematic client versions."},{"key":"gate.startup_log_presence_rate","description":"Rate at which instances emit the expected startup log line indicating the backend is running.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["log.startup.backend_running_on_port"],"segment_dimension":"deployment","rationale":"Confirms successful process start and readiness signaling; missing logs can indicate crash loops or logging misconfiguration."},{"key":"gate.port_discovery_file_write_rate","description":"Rate at which instances successfully attempt to write the port discovery file for the frontend.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["file.write.tmp.todo-backend-port"],"segment_dimension":"service_instance","rationale":"Ensures frontend discovery mechanism is functioning; failures can break connectivity even if the backend is otherwise healthy."},{"key":"gate.port_file_write_to_startup_log_ratio","description":"Ratio of port-file write attempts to startup log events.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["file.write.tmp.todo-backend-port","log.startup.backend_running_on_port"],"segment_dimension":"deployment","rationale":"Detects partial startup where the service runs but discovery file is not written (or vice versa), which can cause environment-specific outages."}]}

gates.description: Availability of the service as measured by successful healthcheck responses.
gates.rationale: A basic liveness signal; drops indicate the service is down, wedged, or failing to serve requests.

gates.description: Overall successful API response rate across core todo endpoints (list/get/create/update/delete).
gates.rationale: Tracks end-to-end API reliability; a falling success ratio indicates regressions, outages, or increased server-side failures.

gates.description: Rate of unhandled internal server errors returned to clients.
gates.rationale: 500s represent defects or infrastructure issues; monitoring rate helps catch crashes, bad deploys, and dependency failures quickly.

gates.description: Ratio of stacktrace logs to 500 responses (should correlate closely).
gates.rationale: Detects logging gaps (missing stacktraces) or noisy logging (stacktraces without corresponding 500s), both of which hinder incident response.

gates.description: Proportion of GET-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.
gates.rationale: Elevated not-found ratios can indicate client misuse, broken ID propagation, data loss, or routing issues.

gates.description: Proportion of UPDATE-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.
gates.rationale: Spikes can indicate stale clients, incorrect IDs, or data consistency problems affecting updates.

gates.description: Proportion of DELETE-todo requests resulting in not-found responses.
gates.rationale: Helps detect client retries against already-deleted items, incorrect IDs, or data integrity issues.

gates.description: Proportion of create requests rejected due to missing/blank title.
gates.rationale: High validation error ratios often indicate UI/API contract regressions, bot traffic, or misuse by specific clients.

gates.description: Proportion of update requests rejected because no updatable fields were provided.
gates.rationale: Catches client-side bugs where empty payloads are sent, and helps pinpoint problematic client versions.

gates.description: Rate at which instances emit the expected startup log line indicating the backend is running.
gates.rationale: Confirms successful process start and readiness signaling; missing logs can indicate crash loops or logging misconfiguration.

gates.description: Rate at which instances successfully attempt to write the port discovery file for the frontend.
gates.rationale: Ensures frontend discovery mechanism is functioning; failures can break connectivity even if the backend is otherwise healthy.

gates.description: Ratio of port-file write attempts to startup log events.
gates.rationale: Detects partial startup where the service runs but discovery file is not written (or vice versa), which can cause environment-specific outages.
